_note: REAL /api/curated/as-degrees?college_id=cc:<n> output for 3 colleges (from the local dev DB, 2026-07-17), for the AS Degrees design session. Cabrillo cc:41 = one clean local CS A.S.; Santa Ana cc:14 = a college with all THREE degree types (shows the multi-degree-per-college case); De Anza cc:113 = quarter-system (90-unit) with an AS-T + a local A.A. KNOWN LIMITATION: the `deviations` field (missing_groups/extra_groups) is currently NAIVE — extracted groups are not yet concept-aligned to the statewide template, so it reports all template groups missing + all local groups extra. Design the deviation UI to the field's INTENT (see the prompt); real values will be sparse (1-3 missing/extra) once alignment lands. Same caveat applies to the `units_mismatch` flag where GE units weren't captured.

requirement_groups.label_seen: Cabrillo College General Education
Recommended Major Requirements (Complete the following number of credits: 14)
Recommended Major Requirements (Complete the following number of credits: 4)
Recommended Major Requirements (Complete the following number of credits: 4)
Recommended Major Electives (Complete the following number of credits: 8)
Computer Engineering Pathway (Complete the following number of credits: 30) — alternative 30-unit major track, plus general electives (any transferable or degree applicable course)

extraction.notes:
Source is the official Cabrillo College eLumen catalog, 2025-2026 (cabrillo.elumenapp.com). The catalog is a JavaScript-only SPA — plain WebFetch returned only a "Catalog" shell, so I rendered it in the browser and transcribed the program page plus each individual course page for exact units. needs_browser=false because full extraction succeeded, but note the source cannot be scraped by a non-JS fetch pipeline.

ONE degree only: Cabrillo has exactly one associate degree literally named "Computer Science" — "Computer Science - Associate in Science (A.S.)" in the Computer Science (CS) department. The separate Computer and Information Systems (CIS) department offers "Computer and Information Systems" degrees (a different name), which I did NOT extract because the survey's "closest local computing degree named Computer Science" resolves to this same CS A.S. Therefore degree_type local_cs_as and local_computing point to the identical program; I report it once (as local_cs_as) rather than duplicating it.

Degree total = 60 semester units: Cabrillo College General Education + at least 30 units in the major. GE follows the local "Cabrillo College General Education" pattern and is not enumerated (recorded as a ge_area group).

The 30-unit major is presented as TWO alternative pathways within the same degree:
(1) Default CS pathway = four subgroups totaling 30 units: [Core 14 units, all required: CS21 + MATH5A + MATH5B] + [4 units, choose ONE programming language: CS19 C++ OR CS20J Java OR CS20P Python] + [4 units, choose ONE discrete math: CS23 OR MATH23 — these are cross-listed equivalents] + [8 units of Recommended Major Electives chosen from CHEM1A/1B, CS24, MATH5C/6/7, PHYS4A/4B/4C, BIO9A/9B].
(2) "Computer Engineering Pathway" = 30 units chosen from a broader list (CS19, CS20J, CS20P, CS21, CS23/MATH23, CS24, MATH5A/5B/5C, MATH6, MATH7, CHEM1A/1B, PHYS4A/4B/4C, BIO9A/9B) plus "General electives: any transferable or degree applicable course." I captured this as a choose_units(30) group labeled "Computer Engineering Pathway".

OR options: CS19/CS20J/CS20P (pick one language); CS23/MATH23 (pick one, equivalent cross-listed Discrete Mathematics). Every per-course unit value was verified directly from that course's own 2025-2026 catalog page. Note MATH6 and MATH7 are 3.0 units (not 4). Degree contains genuine CS/programming courses (CS19/20J/20P/21/23/24), satisfying the CS-content sanity check.

requirement_groups.label_seen: Take ALL of the following courses: Units: 16.0
Select ONE course from the following: Units: 3.0
Select an additional SIX units from the following: Units: 6.0-8.0
General Education (local SAC associate-degree GE pattern) + electives to reach 60-unit minimum

extraction.notes:
Source: Santa Ana College 2025-2026 Catalog PDF (https://sac.edu/admissions/catalogarchive/SAC_Catalog_2025-2026.pdf), catalog pages 119-121 (Computer Science section). The main catalog PDF is compressed and unreadable via WebFetch; extracted successfully with pdftotext -layout. Unit system = semester. The catalog uses a two-column layout.

DEGREE IDENTITIES: SAC offers a degree literally named "Computer Science" both as a local A.S. and as an AS-T. Therefore local_cs_as and local_computing map to the SAME degree (Computer Science: A.S. Degree, Program Code SAC.CMPR.AS, PCN 04197) — I entered it under both degree_type values as instructed ("extract each that exists"); they are identical, not two separate programs. The AS-T is Computer Science: A.S.-T Degree for Transfer (SAC.CMPR.AST, PCN 32396).

TOTAL UNITS: total_units is reported as 60 (the CA associate-degree / ADT minimum degree total incl. GE + electives). The catalog's printed "Total" figures are MAJOR-only: AS-T major = 29.0 units; local A.S. major = 25.0-27.0 units. AS-T explicitly requires 60 CSU-transferable semester units.

AS-T (SAC.CMPR.AST) — major is all 8 courses required (no choose-one options): CMPR 121 (3.0), CMPR 131 (3.0), CMPR 129 (4.0), CMPR 140 (3.0), MATH 180 (4.0), MATH 185 (4.0), PHYS 217 (4.0), PHYS 227 (4.0) = 29.0. GE = Cal-GETC (CSU GE or IGETC may be used in some cases); min 2.0 GPA; grade C/P in each major course; min 18 units in major.

LOCAL A.S. (SAC.CMPR.AS) — 3 major groups: (1) "Take ALL" core 16.0 units: CMPR 100, 120, 121, 129, 131; (2) "Select ONE" 3.0 units: CMPR 112 / CMPR 205 / CMPR 213; (3) "Select an additional SIX units" 6.0-8.0: choose from a 17-course CMPR/MATH list. Note CMPR 112, 205, 213 appear in BOTH group 2 and group 3 in the catalog (a course used to satisfy the choose-one cannot double-count). GE follows SAC local associate-degree GE pattern (Plan A/B/C) plus competency/electives to reach the 60-unit minimum.

Both degrees contain genuine CMPR programming courses (Programming Concepts, Data Structures, etc.), confirming these are the correct Computer Science programs and not a math/physics-only mis-grab. Not to be confused with the separate Computer Information Systems: A.S. (SAC.CIS.AS) or the Computer Science: Certificate of Achievement (SAC.CMPR.CA), which are different programs on adjacent pages.

requirement_groups.label_seen: Required Core: (36.5 Units)
Required Core - Programming - Complete one option: (9 - 13.5 Units)
General Education (Cal-GETC pattern)
Additional Cal-GETC and CSU-transferable electives as needed to reach at least 90 units

extraction.notes:
SOURCE ACCESS: De Anza's official catalog is the eLumen SPA at deanza.elumenapp.com (current published year = 2025-2026). Plain WebFetch and curl to deanza.edu are Cloudflare-blocked (HTTP 403) and the eLumen catalog is JavaScript-only, so I used the browser pane to render and transcribe both program pages. Data is COMPLETE and read directly from the current official catalog, so needs_browser is set false; but note that any non-browser fetcher will be blocked. The stale PDF at deanza.edu/counseling/pdf/degrees/transfer_computer_science.pdf is the 2016-2017 sheet and was NOT used. Unit system is QUARTER (Foothill-De Anza district); both degrees total 90 quarter units (= 60 semester), meeting the CA associate minimum.

CS AS-T (degree_type ast): pulled from https://deanza.elumenapp.com/catalog/2025-2026/program/computer-science-for-transfer-fall-2025 . Contains real CS/programming courses (CIS 21JA assembly, plus CIS 22A/22B/22C or CIS 22C/35A), so it is the correct CS program, not a math/physics-only mis-grab. Two groups: (1) Required Core 36.5 units, all required; (2) 'Required Core - Programming - Complete one option' 9-13.5 units. The programming group is an EITHER/OR of two fixed options, which I modeled as choose_units (units_min 9) over the deduped union of the four courses. Exact options: Option 1 (13.5 u) = CIS 22A + CIS 22B/22BH + CIS 22C/22CH; Option 2 (9 u) = CIS 22C/22CH + CIS 35A. CIS 22C appears in both options but is listed once (deduped). Major total 45.5-50 units. GE follows the Cal-GETC pattern; electives added as needed to reach 90. Many core courses have honors alternates printed as 'X / XH' (MATH 1A/1AH, 1B/1BH, 1C/1CH, 22/22H; CIS 22B/22BH, 22C/22CH) - credit for only one version.

Systems Programming A.A. (degree_type local_computing): pulled from https://deanza.elumenapp.com/catalog/2025-2026/program/systems-programming-fall-2025v2 (this is the A.A. Degree; a separate COA-A version exists at systems-programming-fall-2025). This is the closest LOCAL computing associate degree (De Anza offers no local CS A.S.; the CIS A.A. degrees are Business Programming, Cybersecurity, Database Development Practitioner, Network Administration, Network Programming, and Systems Programming). Three groups: Required Core 32 units (all); List A 4-5 units (choose one course); List B 4.5 units (choose one course). Major total 40.5-41.5 units. GE = De Anza GE or Cal-GETC pattern; must also complete ENGL C1000/C1000H or ESL 5 with C or higher; electives to reach 90. Honors alternates: CIS 22B/22BH, 26B/26BH, 22C/22CH. Both program pages carry a footnote 'Revised on Oct. 1, 2025, to align with current requirements.'
